title: ceanothus divergens var. confusus
intro: Ceanothus divergens var. confusus, also known as California lilac, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and gray-green leaves. The medicinal properties of Ceanothus divergens var. confusus include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high content of alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Ceanothus divergens var. confusus can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves or flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
